revision.text:
'''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | group = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | construction_period = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Tumulus Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular burial mounds. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (some designated Joetsu City Cultural Properties) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|Artifacts from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Cultural Properties === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Joetsu]] It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Tumulus Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular burial mounds. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | group = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | construction_period = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Tumulus Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular burial mounds. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (some designated Joetsu City Cultural Properties) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|Artifacts from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Cultural Properties === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Cultural '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Tumulus (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | group = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | construction_period = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Tumulus Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular burial mounds. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (some designated Joetsu City Cultural Properties) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|Artifacts from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Cultural Properties === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Tumulus Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | group = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | construction_period = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (some designated Joetsu City Cultural Properties) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|Artifacts from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Cultural Properties === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Cultural It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | group = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | construction_period = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (some designated Joetsu City Cultural Properties) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|Artifacts from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Cultural Properties === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Joetsu]] This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | group = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | construction_period = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (some designated Joetsu City Cultural Properties) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|Artifacts from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Cultural Properties === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Joetsu]] It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts (some designated Joetsu City Cultural Properties) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Cultural Properties === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | group = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some designated Joetsu City Cultural Properties) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Cultural Properties === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Cultural Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | group = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some designated Joetsu City Cultural Properties) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Cultural Properties === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Cultural Properties] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Joetsu]] Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some designated Joetsu City other_designation) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_designation === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural other_designation | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_designation === * '''Artifacts from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | designated_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural other_designation | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designated as a Niigata Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable artifacts excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | designation1_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural other_designation | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been designation1 as a Niigata Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some designation1 Joetsu City other_designation) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural other_designation === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_designation === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | other_designation1_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural other_other_designation | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a Niigata Prefectural other_other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some other_designation1 Joetsu City other_other_designation) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural other_other_designation === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_other_designation === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial_chamber = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | other_designation1_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural other_other_designation | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a Niigata Prefectural other_other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some other_designation1 Joetsu City other_other_designation) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural other_other_designation === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_other_designation === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City
