{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | other_designation1_cultural_property = Niigata Prefectural other_designation | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a Niigata Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some other_designation1 Joetsu City other_designation) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural other_designation === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_designation === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City other_designation] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Joetsu]] These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | other_designation1_notes = Niigata Prefectural other_designation | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a Niigata Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some other_designation1 Joetsu City other_designation) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural other_designation === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_designation === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a Niigata Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some other_designation1 Joetsu City other_designation) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural Designation == === Niigata Prefectural other_designation === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_designation === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | other_designation1_notes = Niigata Prefectural other_designation | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a Niigata Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some other_designation1 Joetsu City other_designation) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural other_designation === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_designation === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | other_designation1_notes = Niigata Prefectural other_designation | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been other_designation1 as a Niigata Prefectural other_designation. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some other_designation1 Joetsu City other_designation) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural other_designation === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City other_designation === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Joetsu]] Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|12=draft}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|12=draft}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugawara Kofun}}

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|12=draft}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|12=draft}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugawara Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|12=draft}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|12=draft}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sugawara Kofun}}

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|12=draft}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|12=draft}}. File:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{citation needed|Sugawara Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Sugawara Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]]

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Sugawara Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Sugawara Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]]

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:菅原古墳 up to rev 96036213
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 菅原古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（左に後円部、右に前方部） |別名 = 菅原31号墳/菅原神社古墳 |所属 = 菅原古墳群 |所在地 = [[新潟県]][[上越市]][[清里区]]菅原（字天神林）（菅原神社境内） |緯度度 = 37|緯度分 = 4|緯度秒 = 26.35 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 19|経度秒 = 38.55 |ISO = JP-15 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長29m
高さ1.8m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]前半-中葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 新潟県指定史跡「菅原古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=菅原古墳|mark=前方後円墳-南.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=新潟県内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} '''菅原古墳'''（すがわらこふん/すがはらこふん、菅原31号墳/菅原神社古墳）は、[[新潟県]][[上越市]][[清里区]]菅原にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。菅原古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。新潟県指定[[史跡]]に指定されている。 本項目では、菅原古墳が属する'''[[#菅原古墳群|菅原古墳群]]'''についても解説する。 == 概要 == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|菅原神社社殿（左）と菅原古墳（右）}}]] 新潟県南西部、[[頸城平野]]南東縁の独立丘陵（岡嶺丘陵）上に築造された古墳である。菅原古墳群のうち最南端に位置し、現在は菅原神社（[[延喜式内社]]）境内に所在する。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘表面で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|菅原古墳群（続古墳）|2002}}。墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされる{{Sfn|菅原古墳群（続古墳）|2002}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、初期横穴式石室と推測され、現在は天井石・側壁の一部を露出する{{Sfn|菅原古墳群（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳}}。副葬品は詳らかでない。 この菅原古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]前半-中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|菅原古墳群（続古墳）|2002}}。新潟県内では横穴式石室を有する前方後円墳（古墳時代後期の前方後円墳）としては唯一であるとして重要視される古墳である{{Sfn|菅原古墳群（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳}}。 古墳域は[[1952年]]（[[昭和]]27年）に新潟県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：29メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：18メートル ** 高さ：1.8メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：12メートル ** 幅：15メートル ** 高さ：1メートル File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|後円部の[[横穴式石室]] == 菅原古墳群 == '''菅原古墳群'''は、岡嶺丘陵の東側斜面にある[[古墳群]]（[[群集墳]]）。前方後円墳1基（31号墳）・[[円墳]]100基以上から構成されたが、現在は多くが失われている。 [[江戸時代]]中期には約150基{{Sfn|埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳}}、調査以前には108基の古墳が存在したというが、[[1960年]]（昭和35年）の調査時点では30基のみが確認されている{{Sfn|菅原古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[1909年]]（[[明治]]42年）に[[坪井正五郎]]・梅山寿三郎、[[1929年|1929]]-[[1930年]]（昭和4-5年）に[[後藤守一]]・小松芳春らによって、円墳7基の調査が実施されている{{Sfn|菅原古墳（古墳）|1989}}。調査によれば、埋葬施設は横穴式石室を主体とする古墳群と見られる。また副葬品として、鉄刀・鉄鏃・刀子・馬具・玉類・金環・銀環・アスファルト玉が検出されている{{Sfn|菅原古墳（古墳）|1989}}。営造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]代と推定され、[[宮口古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）・[[水科古墳群]]（国の史跡）などとともに頸城平野東部における後期群集墳の1つになる{{Sfn|埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳}}。 古墳群のうち、上述の31号墳（菅原古墳）は新潟県指定史跡に指定されているほか、1号墳と16号墳石室（清里区総合事務所付近に移転復元：附指定）は上越市指定史跡に指定され、古墳群からの出土品は上越市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 ファイル:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|出土品（一部は上越市指定有形文化財）
{{small|牧歴史民俗資料館展示。}} ファイル:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|出土品（森成コレクション）
{{small|牧歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 新潟県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 菅原古墳 - 所有者は菅原神社。1952年（昭和27年）12月10日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/130374.pdf 新潟県の文化財一覧]}}（新潟県ホームページ）。。 === 上越市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 菅原古墳群出土品（考古資料）[https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-city268.html 菅原古墳群出土品]（上越市ホームページ）。 - 牧歴史民俗資料館保管。 * 史跡 ** 菅原古墳群第1号墳 附 同第16号墳移転復元石室1基[https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-city277.html 菅原古墳群第1号墳 附 同第16号墳移転復元石室1基]（上越市ホームページ）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（上越市教育委員会設置） * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.maibun.net/jigyo/files/page6a/kennai30-sugawara.pdf 「埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳」]}}（新潟県埋蔵文化財調査事業団）|ref={{Harvid|埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳}}}} - リンクは新潟県埋蔵文化財調査事業団。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=菅原古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 15 新潟県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490158|ref={{Harvid|菅原古墳群（平凡社）|1999}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=中島栄一|year=1989|chapter=菅原古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|菅原古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本博文|year=2002|chapter=菅原古墳群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|菅原古墳群（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=菅原古墳群発掘調査報告書 -第6・15・16・28号墳-|publisher=清里村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[宮口古墳群]] - 国の史跡。 * [[水科古墳群]] - 国の史跡。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sugawara Kofun}} * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-pref-kiyosato007.html 菅原古墳] - 上越市ホームページ {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=菅原古墳群 |1-1=新潟県の古墳 |1-2=上越市の歴史 |1-3=群集墳 }} {{デフォルトソート:すかわらこふん}} [[Category:新潟県の古墳]] [[Category:上越市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:新潟県指定史跡]]

菅原古墳}}。副葬品は詳らかでない。 この菅原古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]前半-中葉頃の築造と推定される{{Sfn|菅原古墳群（続古墳）|2002}}。新潟県内では横穴式石室を有する前方後円墳（古墳時代後期の前方後円墳）としては唯一であるとして重要視される古墳である{{Sfn|菅原古墳群（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳}}。 古墳域は[[1952年]]（[[昭和]]27年）に新潟県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 * 墳丘長：29メートル * 後円部 ** 直径：18メートル ** 高さ：1.8メートル * 前方部 ** 長さ：12メートル ** 幅：15メートル ** 高さ：1メートル File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|後円部の[[横穴式石室]] == 菅原古墳群 == '''菅原古墳群'''は、岡嶺丘陵の東側斜面にある[[古墳群]]（[[群集墳]]）。前方後円墳1基（31号墳）・[[円墳]]100基以上から構成されたが、現在は多くが失われている。 [[江戸時代]]中期には約150基{{Sfn|埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳}}、調査以前には108基の古墳が存在したというが、[[1960年]]（昭和35年）の調査時点では30基のみが確認されている{{Sfn|菅原古墳（古墳）|1989}}。[[1909年]]（[[明治]]42年）に[[坪井正五郎]]・梅山寿三郎、[[1929年|1929]]-[[1930年]]（昭和4-5年）に[[後藤守一]]・小松芳春らによって、円墳7基の調査が実施されている{{Sfn|菅原古墳（古墳）|1989}}。調査によれば、埋葬施設は横穴式石室を主体とする古墳群と見られる。また副葬品として、鉄刀・鉄鏃・刀子・馬具・玉類・金環・銀環・アスファルト玉が検出されている{{Sfn|菅原古墳（古墳）|1989}}。営造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]代と推定され、[[宮口古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）・[[水科古墳群]]（国の史跡）などとともに頸城平野東部における後期群集墳の1つになる{{Sfn|埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳}}。 古墳群のうち、上述の31号墳（菅原古墳）は新潟県指定史跡に指定されているほか、1号墳と16号墳石室（清里区総合事務所付近に移転復元：附指定）は上越市指定史跡に指定され、古墳群からの出土品は上越市指定有形文化財に指定されている。 ファイル:菅原古墳群出土品.JPG|出土品（一部は上越市指定有形文化財）
{{small|牧歴史民俗資料館展示。}} ファイル:菅原古墳群出土品 (森成コレクション).JPG|出土品（森成コレクション）
{{small|牧歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 新潟県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 菅原古墳 - 所有者は菅原神社。1952年（昭和27年）12月10日指定{{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.niigata.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/130374.pdf 新潟県の文化財一覧]}}（新潟県ホームページ）。。 === 上越市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 菅原古墳群出土品（考古資料）[https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-city268.html 菅原古墳群出土品]（上越市ホームページ）。 - 牧歴史民俗資料館保管。 * 史跡 ** 菅原古墳群第1号墳 附 同第16号墳移転復元石室1基[https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-city277.html 菅原古墳群第1号墳 附 同第16号墳移転復元石室1基]（上越市ホームページ）。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（上越市教育委員会設置） * {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[https://www.maibun.net/jigyo/files/page6a/kennai30-sugawara.pdf 「埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳」]}}（新潟県埋蔵文化財調査事業団）|ref={{Harvid|埋文にいがたNo.32 菅原古墳}}}} - リンクは新潟県埋蔵文化財調査事業団。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1986|chapter=菅原古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 15 新潟県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490158|ref={{Harvid|菅原古墳群（平凡社）|1999}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=中島栄一|year=1989|chapter=菅原古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|菅原古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=橋本博文|year=2002|chapter=菅原古墳群|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|菅原古墳群（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=菅原古墳群発掘調査報告書 -第6・15・16・28号墳-|publisher=清里村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[宮口古墳群]] - 国の史跡。 * [[水科古墳群]] - 国の史跡。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sugawara Kofun}} * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/cultural-property-pref-kiyosato007.html 菅原古墳] - 上越市ホームページ {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=菅原古墳群 |1-1=新潟県の古墳 |1-2=上越市の歴史 |1-3=群集墳 }} {{デフォルトソート:すかわらこふん}} [[Category:新潟県の古墳]] [[Category:上越市の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:新潟県指定史跡]]
