Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Sugawara Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}}

While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{citation needed|Sugawara Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]]

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaguchi Kofun Group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushina Kofun Group|qq=}}. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Joetsu]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Sugawara Kofun]] [[Category:Shokoku Maki]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]]

It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaguchi Kofun Group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushina Kofun Group|qq=}}. Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|qq=}}, {{ill|Joetsu|ja|上越市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joetsu|qq=}}, Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|ja|宮口古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaguchi Kofun Group|qq=}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|ja|水科古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizushina Kofun Group|qq=}}. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Joetsu]], Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:上越市の歴史]] [[Category:新潟県の古墳]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]]

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Joetsu]], Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|ja|清里区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu|qq=}}, [[:en:Jōetsu, Niigata|Joetsu]], Niigata Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|37|4|26.35|N|138|19|38.55|E|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}}

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Joetsu|qid=Q582289}}; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705}} | coordinates = {{Coord|37.074|N|138.3273888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]][[sn:Sugawara Kofun]]}} [[sn:Sugawara Kofun]] [[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}}

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Joetsu|qid=Q582289}}; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|qid=Q132705}} | coordinates = {{Coord|37.074|N|138.3273888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}}

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Joetsu|WD=Q582289}}; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705}} | coordinates = {{Coord|37.074|N|138.3273888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Joetsu|WD=Q582289}}; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705}} | coordinates = {{Coord|37.074|N|138.3273888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Sugawara Kofun]][[ja:菅原古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q95965925]] Wikidata name: Sugawara Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Joetsu|WD=Q582289}}; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705}} | coordinates = {{Coord|37.074|N|138.3273888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q95965925}}

It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Joetsu|WD=Q582289}}; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705}} | coordinates = {{Coord|37.074|N|138.3273888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the [[:en:Miyaguchi Kofun Group|Miyaguchi Kofun Group]] and the [[:en:Mizushina Kofun Group|Mizushina Kofun Group]]. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
