[[co:commons:Category:Sugawara Kofun]][[ja:菅原古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Sugawara Kofun]][[ja:菅原古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sugawara Kofun]][[ja:菅原古墳]] '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Joetsu|WD=Q582289}}; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705}} | coordinates = {{Coord|37.074|N|138.3273888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|en|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|WD=Q11453446|lt=Miyaguchi Kofun Group}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|en|Mizushina Kofun Group|WD=Q11549051|lt=Mizushina Kofun Group}}. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|en|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|WD=Q11453446|lt=Miyaguchi Kofun Group}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|en|Mizushina Kofun Group|WD=Q11549051|lt=Mizushina Kofun Group}}. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Sugawara Kofun]][[ja:菅原古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Sugawara Kofun]][[ja:菅原古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sugawara Kofun]][[ja:菅原古墳]] '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳) {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Sugawara Kofun | image = [[File:Sugawara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (rear circular section on the left, front square section on the right) | alternate_name = Sugawara 31 Kofun / Sugawara Shrine Kofun | part_of = Sugawara Kofun Cluster | location = {{ill|Joetsu|WD=Q582289|1=Jōetsu, Niigata}}; {{ill|Niigata Prefecture|WD=Q132705|1=Niigata Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|37.074|N|138.3273888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | length = 29m (total length), 1.8m (rear height) | burial = Horizontal stone chamber | built = Early to mid-6th century | Historic Site1_notes = Niigata Prefectural Historic Site | map_caption = location within Niigata Prefecture | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Sugawara Kofun''' (菅原古墳), also known as Sugawara 31 Kofun or Sugawara Shrine Kofun, is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun) located in Kiyosato-ku, Joetsu, Niigata Prefecture, Japan. It is part of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster and has been Historic Site1 as a Niigata Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Sugawara-jinja (Joetsu) and Sugawara Kofun.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Sugawara Shrine (left) and Sugawara Kofun (right)}}]] Sugawara Kofun was constructed on the southeastern edge of the Myōkō Plain, atop the independent Okamine Hills. It represents the southernmost kofun in the Sugawara Kofun Cluster, situated within the precincts of Sugawara Shrine, an ancient Shinto shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. The mound features a keyhole-shaped tumulus oriented southward. The mound lacks evidence of haniwa (ceramic figurines) or fukiishi (covering stones). Surrounding the mound is a moat, while the burial chamber consists of an early type of horizontal stone chamber, some parts of which, including ceiling stones and sidewalls, remain exposed. The kofun is estimated to have been built during the early to mid-6th century. It is the only known keyhole-shaped tumulus in Niigata Prefecture containing a horizontal stone chamber, making it an important archaeological site. == Kofun Specifications == * Total length: 29 meters * Rear circular section: ** Diameter: 18 meters ** Height: 1.8 meters * Front square section: ** Length: 12 meters ** Width: 15 meters ** Height: 1 meter File:Sugawara Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front section toward the rear File:Sugawara Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear section toward the front File:Sugawara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|Rear section with exposed horizontal stone chamber == Sugawara Kofun Cluster == The '''Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' consists of over 100 tumuli, including one keyhole-shaped tumulus (31 Kofun) and numerous circular Kofuns. While over 150 mounds were recorded in the Edo period, only 30 were confirmed during surveys conducted in 1960. Notable burial goods excavated from the cluster include iron swords, arrowheads, ornamental beads, and jewelry, indicating its significance during the 6th century. These findings align the cluster with other important burial sites such as the {{ill|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|en|Miyaguchi Kofun Group|WD=Q11453446|lt=Miyaguchi Kofun Group|1=Miyaguchi Kofun Group}} and the {{ill|Mizushina Kofun Group|en|Mizushina Kofun Group|WD=Q11549051|lt=Mizushina Kofun Group|1=Mizushina Kofun Group}}. File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations (some Historic Site1 Joetsu City Historic Site) File:菅原古墳群Excavated Artifacts (森成コレクション).JPG|burial goods from the Mori Collection == Cultural designation1 == === Niigata Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Sugawara Kofun''' - Designated on December 10, 1952. Ownership resides with Sugawara Shrine. === Joetsu City Historic Site === * '''burial goods from the Sugawara Kofun Cluster''' - Stored at the Maki Historical Folklore Museum. * '''1st and 16th Kofun stone chambers (relocated and restored near the Kiyosato Community Office)''' == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Literature == * {{Cite book|title=Excavation Report of the Sugawara Kofun Cluster|editor=Kiyosato Village Education Board|year=2000}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.joetsu.niigata.jp/site/cultural-property/sugawara-kofun.html Sugawara Kofun - Joetsu City Historic Site] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|菅原古墳|version=96036213|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:菅原古墳]] {{Commons category|Sugawara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q95965925}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Joetsu, Niigata]] [[Category:Kofun of Niigata prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
